worked: Grounding before building — finding `lib/matchers/` first avoided rebuilding an embedding stack from scratch and resolved the "no training" concern with evidence.

The repl-agent comparison surfaced the single best idea: **confidence as a property of retrieval (score + margin-to-#2), not the model's self-report.** The live demo (LLM 0.95 vs retrieval 0.643 on a 0.0055-margin tie) is the proof.
Skeptical-review (fresh adversarial context) caught real nits and verified the Pinecone-7.3.0 result shapes by instantiating the real OpenAPI objects.
didnt_work: File Tools MCP `list_sheets`/`read_tabular` errored on .xlsx (`XLSX.readFile is not a function`) → pandas fallback.

A tracked-file edit (registration in `methods/__init__.py`) was LOST across a user commit (58049cdc) — had to re-add. Watch for collateral loss of uncommitted edits to tracked files across commits/merges.
Early over-defensive code (dict-or-attr branches) tripped my own no-speculative-breadth rule; skeptical-review flagged it.
gotchas: `NonRetryablePipelineError` is NOT special-cased by `task_runner` — despite the name it gets RETRIED. `TaskTerminalError(code, ...)` is the only true terminal/no-retry path; it must be passed through `process_pipeline` (now via `except WorkerException`).
Pinecone cache backend in prod = `MongoDBKVBackend` (DEBUG → in-memory), not Redis.
The autocorrect hinter mangles `rg`/`cat` OUTPUT (pinecone→"ln", get_object→"n") — read files with the Read tool, don't trust mangled bash text.
The retrieval_confidence formula is cosine-scale; do NOT copy repl-agent's `/5`,`/10` lexical-scale constants.
feedback: Wants to think-first: explore/ground/recommend before changing code; don't jump to edits.
Comment style: terse, human-first, no archeology/planning artifacts (the pyproject thread).
Show how to run/test things themselves (the "where's the output / show me how to test" correction).
Strongly anti-overengineering; measure before committing; wants the easy wins + tradeoffs + risks made explicit.

pending: **Accuracy benchmark** (highest priority, recurring theme): wire a labeled, DIVERSE sample into `tests/benchmark_classifier.py`; compare gate sources (agent vs retrieval vs min) and plain vs two-pass. This is the gate to trusting `retrieval`/`min` confidence and to calibrating the retrieval_confidence formula (currently an uncalibrated cosine-scale heuristic).
**Two-pass recall fallback** — designed, NOT built (`retry_shortlist_size` arg: on low pass-1 confidence, re-retrieve with larger K, re-classify, then gate).
**Full pipeline-runner end-to-end** — the job-config path (process_pipeline → data_set_bulk → output columns) has NOT been run; only direct matcher+agent + isolated execute() verified.
**Decide which stashed wins to keep/merge** — deterministic confidence + alternatives + traceability are stashed.
**S3 catalog-load path** (`init_index_from_s3`) not live-tested (direct test used `init_index` from the local XLSX).
Stale ref: job config `dev_notes` points at `scripts/upload_and_index_client_part_types.py`, which the user removed.
Hold behind benchmark: hybrid lexical re-rank, LLM-skip-when-confident, catalog versioning (namespace/hash per list version).
